summary: Section 7.12 (Evidence Exhibits A and B: The 2003 Halloween Storm) already exists in part7 and is published. It comprehensively addresses all curmudgeon concerns: r=0.992 correlation (autocorrelation/DOF, shared atmospheric driver), AI=1.152 asymmetry (expected storm asymmetry, no null distribution), Administrative Mask conspiracy framing (JPL D-22027, noise-cancelling analogy), Forensic Zoom chart forensics (GRACE data product naming, orbit geometry impossibility), and kappa=1.67 circularity (tautological code validation, WIN-013/014 contradiction). WIN-012 detail_evidence also includes new graph forensics analysis. The duplicate paragraph noted by curmudgeon has already been fixed. No new expansion output needed — the coverage gap identified in the review has been filled.
